articles.title: From Genes to Machines: PERA’s First Full-Committee Hearing
articles.content:
丹尼斯·克劳奇

2026年7月14日，美国参议院司法委员会全体举行听证会，题为“从基因到机器：专利资格辩论”。主席查克·格拉斯利表示，这是整个委员会首次，而非参议员汤姆·蒂利斯的知识产权小组委员会，审议《2025年专利资格恢复法案》（S.1546，第119届国会，2025年，配套法案H.R.3152）。PERA旨在废除最高法院在Alice案和Mayo案中扩大的第101条司法例外，并用狭窄法定清单取代不能专利的事项。没有安排投票或修订日程，格拉斯利坦言许多成员仍在研究该法案，尚未支持。

蒂利斯将于本届国会结束时退休，表示愿意修改PERA中关于人类基因的措辞，并请最反对该法案的证人黛布拉·伦纳德提交措辞，以在她看来保护Myriad案。

四名证人作证，意见均等。安德烈·扬库（前美国专利商标局局长）和苏·佩斯金（老龄化研究联盟）敦促通过该法案。J·约翰·李（计算机和通信行业协会）和伦纳德博士（佛蒙特大学病理学荣誉教授，分子病理学协会前主席，Myriad案的指定原告）要求委员会维持现行法律不变。我认为伦纳德博士关于基因专利的讨论已过时——淘金热时代已经结束。她1990年代授权信涉及的孤立天然序列早已被测序并发表，且相关专利已过期，因此任何新主张都会因缺乏新颖性或非显而易见性而失败，无论Myriad在资格门槛上如何主张。如今值得主张的是另一方面，即她本人认可的工程构建物，如CRISPR编辑和基因疗法，以及Myriad已视为合格的cDNA。但她也提到了与人类健康相关的基本关联——诊断发现仍是研究的肥沃领域，且大多被Mayo案阻断。
articles.summary:
by Dennis Crouch

On July 14, 2026, the full Senate Judiciary Committee held its hearing "From Genes to Machines: The Patent Eligibility Debate," which Chairman Chuck Grassley described as the first time the whole committee, rather than Senator Thom Tillis's Intellectual Property Subcommittee, has taken up the Patent Eligibility Restoration Act of 2025, S. 1546, 119th Cong. (2025) (companion H.R. 3152). PERA would erase the judicial exceptions to Section 101 that the Supreme Court expanded in Alice and Mayo, and replace them with a narrow statutory list of things that cannot be patented. No vote or markup was scheduled, and Grassley was candid that many members are still studying the bill (i.e., do not yet support the legislation).

Tillis, who is retiring at the end of this Congress, said he is willing to change PERA's human-gene language, and he asked Debra Leonard, the witness most opposed to aspects of the bill to send him wording that would, in her view, preserve Myriad.

Four witnesses testified, split evenly. Andrei Iancu (former USPTO Director) and Sue Peschin (Alliance for Aging Research) urged passage. J. John Lee (Computer and Communications Industry Association) and Dr. Leonard (Professor Emerita of Pathology at the University of Vermont and a past president of the Association for Molecular Pathology, the named plaintiff in Myriad) asked the committee to leave current law alone.  I found Dr. Leonard's discussion of gene patenting outdated at this point - that gold rush is over.  The isolated natural sequences behind her 1990s licensing letters have long since been sequenced and published, and those patents are expired, so a fresh claim to one would fail for want of novelty or nonobviousness, whatever Myriad says at the eligibility threshold. What is worth claiming today runs the other way, toward the engineered constructs she herself credited, CRISPR edits and gene therapies, and the cDNA that Myriad already treats as eligible.  But, she also mentioned basic correlations associated with human health - the diagnostic discoveries that continue to be ripe areas for research and that are largely blocked by Mayo.

To continue reading, become a Patently-O member. Already a member? Simply log in to access the full post.

articles.title: The Survivors: IPR Petition Filings Stay Low and Highly Targeted in 2026
articles.content:
这是对我5月份帖子关于IPR请愿书数量大幅下降的快速跟进。参见《2026年 inter partes审查》（2026年5月24日）。上图显示的数据截至2026年7月中旬。结果很简单——IPR请愿书数量没有反弹，较2025年前的正常水平下降约70%。

但低不等于零，剩余的请愿书值得仔细审视。驱动因素众所周知：自2025年10月以来，局长约翰·斯夸尔斯（John Squires）亲自控制每一项受理决定，并在实质性审查之前，根据《美国法典》第35编第314(a)条以裁量理由驳回大多数请愿书。他在Magnolia Medical Technologies, Inc.诉Kurin, Inc.一案（IPR2026-00097，局长2026年5月14日）中的先例决定明确阐述了理由：IPR应该是地区法院诉讼的替代方案，而非补充。提出者做出了理性回应。如果一项请愿书具有较差的裁量特征（平行的地区法院案件、重复挑战、外国制造的被控侵权产品），无论现有技术多么强大，都会在门槛阶段被驳回，那么花费金钱提交它几乎没有理由。剩下的只是一个规模较小、自我筛选的高度针对性请愿池。
articles.summary:
by Dennis Crouch

This is a quick follow up from my May post about the dramatic fall-off in IPR petition filings. See Inter Partes Review in 2026 (May 24, 2026). The chart above shows data through mid-July 2026.  And the result is simple – IPR petition filings are not bouncing back. They are running about 70 percent below the pre-2025 norm.

But low is not zero, and the petitions that remain are worth a closer look. The driver is familiar: since October 2025 Director John Squires has personally controlled every institution decision and has denied a large majority of petitions on discretionary grounds under 35 U.S.C. § 314(a), before any merits review. His precedential decision in Magnolia Medical Technologies, Inc. v. Kurin, Inc., IPR2026-00097 (Dir. May 14, 2026), states the rationale plainly: IPR should be an alternative to district court litigation, not an addition to it. Petitioners have responded rationally. If a petition with a weak discretionary profile (a parallel district court case, a repeat challenge, a foreign-manufactured accused product) will be turned away at the threshold no matter how strong the prior art, there is little reason to spend the money filing it. What is left is a smaller, self-selected pool of highly targeted petitions.
